title: Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis
what_description: Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis is a plant variety within the species Roldana kerberi, belonging to the family Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb with a woody base, typically 0.5 to 1.5 meters tall, with a simple or branched stem and a leafy rosette at the base. The leaves are 10-30 cm long, lanceolate or elliptical in shape, and have a glabrous to pubescent upper surface and a pubescent lower surface. The inflorescence is a panicle or a corymb, bearing 10-30 capitula, each with 10-20 yellow or orange flowers, surrounded by a 6-12 mm long, phyllary ring. The plant is endemic to the states of Jalisco and Nayarit in Mexico.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis are inflammation, digestive issues, skin irritation, and wound healing. Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis has been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, effectively treating fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm. This plant also displays antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can aid in pain relief, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal issues. Additionally, it contains antioxidants, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress relief, as well as fever, sleep disorders, and skin conditions like eczema. Furthermore, Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis has been found to possess antiviral properties, making it effective against bacterial infections, insect bites, and even arthritis.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is believed to be responsible for its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are thought to be mediated by the presence of flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis has also been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which are likely due to the presence of volatile oils and other secondary metabolites that can disrupt microbial membranes and inhibit their growth. Additionally, the plant's antifungal properties are thought to be mediated by the presence of phenolic compounds, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce their virulence. Its analgesic properties are likely due to the presence of terpenoids and other bioactive compounds that can interact with pain receptors and reduce pain perception.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis are flavonoids and phenolic acids, specifically quercetin, caffeic acid, luteolin, apigenin, and kaempferol. These compounds have been isolated and identified through gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (GC-MS) and high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) analysis. Quercetin and kaempferol have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while caffeic acid has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties. Luteolin and apigenin have been found to have anti-inflammatory and anticancer activities, with the latter also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The presence of these bioactive compounds in Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis supports its traditional use in folk medicine for treating various ailments, including inflammation and infections.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis are primarily used for their medicinal and cosmetic properties, with notable preparations including tea, which is prepared by infusing the plant in hot water to treat digestive issues, infusion, a similar process to tea, used for treating fever and respiratory problems. Tincture, a concentrated liquid extract of the plant, is used to treat various ailments including inflammation and pain relief. Salve, a topical application made from the plant's leaves, is used to treat skin irritations and wounds, while decoction, a concentrated liquid made by boiling the plant, is used to treat fever and respiratory problems. These preparations are often used in traditional medicine due to the plant's ant-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the efficacy of these preparations.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis improperly include experiences allergic reactions, which can be life-threatening in severe cases. Developing skin irritation is another possible side effect, as the plant's sap or oils may cause skin redness, itching, and inflammation. Ingesting Roldana kerberi var. manantlanensis can cause diarrhea due to its toxic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones. Furthermore, chronic kidney damage is a potential risk if the plant's toxins are absorbed into the bloodstream over an extended period. This is because the plant's toxic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, can be nephrotoxic and cause damage to kidney cells.
